title: NBA insider: Celtics avoiding this ‘elephant in the room’ during Giannis Antetokounmpo pursuit
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics Jaylen Brown would reportedly need to be a part of any deal involving the Celtics landing Antetokounmpo.

Giannis Antetokounmpo AP Photo/Aaron Gash The Celtics are reportedly on the shortlist of teams to watch in the Giannis Antetokounmpo sweepstakes, and it’s not exactly clear yet just how much Milwaukee would need to get back in return for the two-time league-MVP.
According to The Athletic’s Sam Amick, Boston’s interest in pursuing Antetokounmpo is largely based around a 1-for-1 swap of star players Antetokounmpo and the Celtics’ Jaylen Brown.

title: MBTA GM Phil Eng accused of pulling employee’s hair
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News The incident reportedly came after a group discussion about hair, during which Eng poked fun at his own baldness.
Interim Transportation Secretary and MBTA General Manager Phil Eng spoke at a renovated Foxborough Station ahead of the World Cup.

Mark Stockwell for The Boston Globe, File By Abby Patkin June 19, 2026 | 11:20 AM 1 minute to read MBTA General Manager Phillip Eng, the state’s top transportation official, has apologized for an “insensitive interaction” he had during a 2024 team dinner where he allegedly pulled another T employee’s hair.

title: Man allegedly exposed himself inside parked car near Mass. suburban high school
author: John Waller
bullets: Crime The incident was first reported by students who were returning to school from lunch.

By John Waller June 19, 2026 | 11:03 AM 1 minute to read A 39-year-old Arlington man was arrested Thursday after allegedly exposing himself in a car parked near the high school in town the previous week.
Luis Boston is facing a charge of open and gross lewdness, Arlington police said in a release.
